methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $500M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $5.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Commonwealth Fusion Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series B), post-money $5B+

private.name: Sage Geosystems
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series B), post-money $400M

private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series B), post-money $120M

data.narrative:
Pano AI is a Series B-stage climate and energy company founded by Sonia Kastner that has raised $89M to date against a last post-money valuation of $500M+. The valuation assessment draws on a peer universe of five same-sector, same-stage private companies (Commonwealth Fusion Systems, Sage Geosystems, Reverion, CarbonCapture Inc., and Cemvita Factory) alongside one public comparable.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method, using a 1.9x step-up multiplier from the last Series B post-money valuation of $500M, implies a forward valuation of $950M. The peer-median method benchmarks Pano AI against five contemporaneous climate-tech companies at Series B, which trade at a median valuation of $310M, with a range spanning $120M to $5.0B. This wide dispersion reflects meaningful heterogeneity in the peer set, particularly the Commonwealth Fusion outlier at $5.0B.

Triangulating these methods yields a valuation range of $264M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $630M. The current $500M+ post-money sits near the low end of this range, suggesting modest upside to the $630M median under normalized peer comps, though stage-progression logic supports movement toward the $950M level. Key uncertainties include the appropriate comparability weight given the peer set's wide dispersion and whether Commonwealth Fusion's scale generalizes to other climate-tech modalities.
